description: The Health and Social Care Act 2012, introduced by Andrew Lansley under the UK Coalition government, aimed to decentralize the NHS by shifting power to GP-led consortia. It faced massive backlash from medical professionals and the public, leading to a 'pause and listen' period before eventually passing into law.

key_events.description: Lansley announces the white paper outlining 'Liberating the NHS'.
Royal College of General Practitioners officially opposes the bill.
Government initiates a mandatory 'listening exercise' to address concerns.
The Health and Social Care Act receives Royal Assent despite intense opposition.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring how institutional professional opposition impacts public perception of technocratic reform.
